item.title: Bounces forever
item.content:encoded: In the article, it states that the blob will bounce for a potentially indefinite amount of time, which seems kind of ridiculous, even for something anomalous. What if the paint left a small amount on whatever surface it bounces off of, slowly painting the room until it hits somewhere it already has impacted. I was also kind of sad you didn't do test logs on what it can go through and how tough it is. +1 for the concept

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"10-gallon" USE METRIC. I hate the Imperial system, the Foundation uses metric. NEVER. USE. IMPERIAL.
Just a bulltproof paint that also works as a bullet, not interesting enough, and using an imperial measurement just makes the situation worse.
-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't remember how I originally found this article, but I remember not feeling strongly about it one way or the other- it's well done and interesting to think about but didn't get any particular reaction.

This time I found it by going through the index, which meant that I read the title before being reintroduced to the object.

+1, you witty motherfucker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, what happens if the paint hits organic material? I don't get it. I was looking for some clue the whole time, but there wasn't even so much as a [DATA EXPUNGED]. That was what was going through my head for about half the article.

In hindsight, I now realize that it goes through organic material instead of bouncing off. The thing is, the way the thing was written, I thought it stopped when it hit organic material, rather than ignoring it. I'm just saying, there's no reason not to spell out exactly what happens when it comes into contact with organic material; forcing readers to guess only inspires frustration.

Another thing that bothers me is that it can be painted onto things like paint. It feels like it should resist being painted onto things, if it only stops ricocheting when it hits more of itself. I mean, there is some impact when a paintbrush touches a surface. But maybe I'm just missing the point.

+1, in any case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(NOTE: This is based on reputation only, I have no idea if this what he's really like)
I have a feeling there used to be 20 gallons, until Clef painted his basement.

EDIT: P.S.
I believe EchoFourDelta is the guy to ask for balistics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted because I'm hoping that we'll get some test logs if enough upvotes are received. MAKE IT HAPPEN

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
to a uniform speed of 490 m/s, equivalent to a .357 magnum handgun round.

This doesn't set right with me.
Bullets have different velocities based on the manufacturer of the bullet, the weapon the round is fired from, and the specific type of round. (i.e. FMJ, JFP, HP, SWC, Match, etc.), so using a bullet to demonstrate speed doesn't seem ideal.
Of course, there's also the distinct possibility that I'm just an idiot and it's fine as is, but I wanted to say something about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once applied to a surface and allowed to dry, SCP-1134-1 has been observed to be completely bulletproof, withstanding impacts up to a .30 caliber sniper round.

One nitpick:

Rifle ammunition performance is highly dependent upon the weapon firing it. A .30-06 round can be used in a light machine gun with surprisingly devastating results. I don't believe the term 'sniper' is necessary here.

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/.30-06_Springfield

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is doing moderately well, so it must just be us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Roget noticed that too. I don't know what to do about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tone still feels off. Also, I read "Ballistics grade paint", immediately thought of Portal 2, and had no reason not to continue thinking of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changes have improved this, definitely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incorporated. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is propulsion gel from portal 2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hopefully this edit clears that up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the paint bullets multiply and ricochet indefinitely until they hit a surface treated with the paint, wouldn't the guy who flung the paint mention that bit in his call or get shot himself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think we need to install this as part of the "Oh shit Able just woke up" defense grid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not_sure_if_yours_joke,_but_mine_was_joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gay furries with tickle fetishes and the women who accidentally murder each other for them during horrifying paintbrush-play accidents! Next, on Maury!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
if it was the unnaturally good paint you say it is, it wouldn't drip.

come on, dude. he has to maintain believability here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Contact with organic matter paints them completely violet and makes them dance the mating dance of their Cooakawa forefathers."

Don't use expunged like that, especially at the end of a paragraph where there are no context clues to formulate anything other than what I guessed it did. Speaking of which, if you paint a person, do they become bulletproof?

Also, if it was the unnaturally good paint you say it is, it wouldn't drip.

Not voting on this yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gay furries with tickle fetishes and the women who love them! Next, on Maury!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Especially the bit about calling him a fag (which is what I presume "friendly ribbing over his sexual preferences" meant).

dude could be a furry. or he could have a tickle fetish, and they're literally ribbing him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. Ditch the recovery log, it's not always necessary to have one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a moderately okay article that I was "meh"ing over, not gonna vote one way or the other…

…then there was this:

Recovery Log 1134 Abstract: Recovered following interception of a 911 call, in which the caller claimed that he had just "shot Juan and Nick with my fucking paintbrush!" An ambulance from the Special Care Providers front company was dispatched to the scene, ██07 ██th Street, ███████, Illinois. Foundation agents determined that the caller, Henry ██████, had been painting the wall of a new home when his coworkers, Nicholas ██████ and Juan ██████ had engaged in friendly ribbing of Henry over his sexual preferences. Henry responded by flicking SCP-1134-1 at them with a paint brush, resulting in [DATA EXPUNGED]

So. Very. Narmy. Especially the bit about calling him a fag (which is what I presume "friendly ribbing over his sexual preferences" meant). Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: there's too much going on. it's magic paint, it's magic paint that reflects gunfire, it's magic paint that IS gunfire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Say something like "…quickly accelerate to a speed measured at a uniform 490 metres per second, comparable to a .357 Magnum round." (BTW it's .357, not .347.) Drop the next sentence about ricochet behaviour, the paragraph that follows covers that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are some sloppy expungements.

Contact with organic matter [DATA EXPUNGED]
resulting in [DATA EXPUNGED]

At least put periods after them like sentences, or something…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm.

Points in favor:

- Interesting concept
- Writing is good
- Tone is good

Points against:

- Sort of formulaic "does something good/something bad" approach
- Why does it matter why the guy was flicking paint at his friends? Dissonant note in an otherwise good clinical feel

No vote for now, but I'm curious to see what edits you come up with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's actually the first effect I came up with and then the bulletproof characteristics came organically after that. Thanks for the input, I'll try to make that more clear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not that proficient in ballistics. Many people on this site seem to be though. If someone could let me know a better way to describe it, I'd appreciate that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1134-1 appears to be a glossy violet in color, measuring at a visible wavelength of 416 nm.

Just say it's violet. We'll get the picture.

SCP-1134-1 is remarkably efficient when used as a paint. It spreads uniformly and only adheres to surfaces not currently coated in itself. It comes cleanly off of the brush onto the surface, allowing all of the liquid to be utilized with utmost effectiveness.

Just say it's high-quality paint. You don't have to explain how well it comes off the brush.

Any "spills" or "drips"…quickly accelerate to a speed and force equivalent to a .347 magnum handgun round…and behave with trajectories identical to the ricochet characteristics of .347 magnum handgun rounds.

Condense that to be less repetitive.

Contact with organic matter [DATA EXPUNGED]

It shoots them. Just say it shoots them.

Like Boa said, I like this idea, but the execution kills it. Not enough for me to downvote it, but enough to keep me from upvoting. Work out the kinks, and I'll be happy to give you a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this pretty well, but calling out a specific caliber of handgun ammunition as the equivalent seems a bit … off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Namely the drops turning into bullets, it's poorly described and somehow it comes after you emphasize that the paint spreads remarkably well and forms a 100% barrier (bullets do nothing to it or the wall behind it, fire does nothing to it, etc) and it honestly feels like it was tacked on as a downside to an otherwise amazing and incredibly useful product.

If you expanded upon that section a little more so it didn't feel as shallow it would be better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: S'aight. Also, what color is it? Is it ##366?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What features do you feel need to be tightened up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll take that into advisement tomorrow after I get some sleep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quick, Simple, and to the point. Nice effects of the paint. Might want to consider adding fluff such as experiment logs though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea but the execution is lacking, in my opinion.
